documents.title: Getting Started
documents.description: Get started with TraceRTM in minutes
documents.content: # Getting Started with TraceRTM Welcome to TraceRTM! This guide will help you get up and running with the platform. ## Prerequisites Before you begin, ensure you have: - Python 3.12 or higher - Node.js 20 or higher - PostgreSQL 14 or higher (or SQLite for development) - Bun package manager (for frontend development) ## Installation ### 1. Clone the Repository ### 2. Backend Setup The API will be available at . ### 3. Frontend Setup The web app will be available at . ## Next Steps - CLI Tutorial: Learn how to use the CLI - Contributing Guide: How to contribute - Architecture Overview: Understand the system design ## Quick Links | Resource | Description | |----------|-------------| | API Documentation | Interactive API docs | | CLI Tutorial | Command-line interface guide | | MCP Tools | Model Context Protocol integration |

documents.title: Contributing Guide
documents.description: How to contribute to TraceRTM
documents.content: # Contributing to TraceRTM **Last Updated:** 2025-11-21 --- ## Getting Started 1. Fork the repository 2. Clone your fork 3. Create a feature branch 4. Make your changes 5. Write tests 6. Submit a pull request --- ## Test Requirements ### Test Naming Convention All tests must follow the TC-X.Y.Z naming convention: ### Test Function Naming ### Test Template Use the template in for all new tests. ### Test Organization ### Test Traceability Every test must be linked to: 1. **Functional Requirement (FR)** - What requirement does it test? 2. **User Story** - Which story does it implement? 3. **Test Type** - Unit, Integration, or E2E? 4. **Priority** - P0 (critical), P1 (important), P2 (nice-to-have)? ### Test Coverage Requirements - Minimum 80% code coverage - All FRs must have test coverage - All stories must have test coverage - Mix of positive, negative, and edge case tests --- ## Running Tests ### Run All Tests ### Run Specific Test File ### Run Specific Test ### Run with Coverage ### Ru

documents.title: Team Onboarding
documents.description: Complete onboarding guide for new team members
documents.content: # Team Onboarding Guide Welcome to the Trace RTM project! This guide will help you get up to speed quickly. ## What is Trace RTM? Trace is a comprehensive multi-view project management system optimized for power users orchestrating 1000+ AI agents. It provides: - **16 views** covering all PM aspects (Feature, Code, Test, API, etc.) - **Git-style versioning** with time-travel capability - **NATS-based event coordination** for distributed task execution - **Natural language assertions** and verification systems - **Comprehensive traceability** with 60+ link types ## Quick Start ### 1. Set Up Your Environment ### 2. Start Development Servers ### 3. Verify Installation - Backend: http://localhost:8000/docs - Frontend: http://localhost:5173 - Docs: http://localhost:3001 ## Key Architectural Decisions ### Why NATS Over Redis? | Feature | NATS JetStream | Redis | |---------|----------------|-------| | Message Delivery | At-least-once, exactly-once ✅ | At-most-once ❌ | | Persistence | Built-in

documents.content: # Database Guide TraceRTM uses PostgreSQL as the primary database with SQLAlchemy ORM and Alembic for migrations. ## Database Schema ### Core Tables #### Items Table Stores all traceability items: #### Links Table Stores relationships between items: ## Migrations ### Creating Migrations ### Migration File ### Running Migrations ## Querying ### Basic Queries ### Joins ### Eager Loading ### Aggregations ## Indexes ### Creating Indexes ### Index Usage ## Performance Optimization ### Query Optimization ### Connection Pooling ### Batch Operations ## JSONB Queries PostgreSQL JSONB support for flexible metadata: ## Full-Text Search ## Backup and Restore ### Backup ### Restore ### Automated Backups Add to crontab: ## Monitoring ### Query Performance ### Connection Stats ### Table Statistics ## Maintenance ### VACUUM ### REINDEX ## Best Practices 1. **Use transactions** - Wrap related operations 2. **Add indexes** - For frequently queried columns 3. **Eager load relationships** - Avoid N+1 quer

documents.title: Testing Guide
documents.description: Comprehensive testing strategy for TraceRTM
documents.content: # Testing Guide TraceRTM uses a multi-layered testing approach to ensure code quality and reliability. ## Testing Pyramid ## Backend Testing ### Unit Tests Test individual functions and classes in isolation. **Location**: **Tools**: pytest, pytest-asyncio, pytest-mock ### Integration Tests Test API endpoints with real database. **Location**: **Tools**: pytest, httpx, TestClient ### Running Backend Tests ## Frontend Testing ### Unit Tests Test individual components and hooks. **Location**: **Tools**: Vitest, Testing Library ### Hook Testing ### Running Frontend Tests ## End-to-End Tests Test complete user flows in a real browser. **Location**: **Tools**: Playwright ### Running E2E Tests ## Test Fixtures ### Backend Fixtures ### Frontend Fixtures ## Mocking ### Backend Mocking ### Frontend Mocking ## Coverage ### Backend Coverage ### Frontend Coverage ## CI/CD Integration ### GitHub Actions ## Best Practices 1. **Write tests first** (TDD) - Think through requirements 2. **Test behavior,

documents.title: Configuration Precedence
documents.description: Understanding TraceRTM configuration
documents.content: # TraceRTM Config Precedence & Locations TraceRTM resolves configuration from multiple sources. This guide explains **where config lives** and **how precedence is applied**. ## Precedence Order Highest → Lowest: 1. **CLI flags** (per-command overrides) 2. **Environment variables** () 3. **Repo project config** () 4. **User project config** () 5. **Global config** () Notes: - Repo project config is intended to be **git‑tracked** and shared. - User project config is for **per‑user overrides**. - Global config is the **fallback** for all projects. ## Locations ### Global (user) - ### Repo project (shared) - ### User project (per‑project per‑user) - ### Local state (ignored) - for db/tmp/cache files ## Example: Repo Project Config ## How Resolution Works - CLI and env override everything. - If key exists in , it is used for all users. - If a user sets a per‑project override in , it wins over the repo config. - If no project config is present, the global config applies. ## Tips - Keep only

documents.title: Migration Guide
documents.description: Guide for migrating data from external systems to TraceRTM
documents.content: # Migration Guide This guide covers migrating requirements, test cases, and traceability links from external systems into TraceRTM. ## Supported Sources TraceRTM can import data from: - **Jira** - Issues, epics, sprints - **GitHub** - Issues, pull requests, projects - **Spreadsheets** - CSV, Excel files - **ReqIF** - Requirements Interchange Format - **Markdown/MDX** - Documentation files ## Migration Process ### 1. Prepare Source Data Export data from your current system: ### 2. Transform Data Map source fields to TraceRTM schema: ### 3. Import Data Use the CLI to import: ## Jira Migration ### Export from Jira ### Field Mapping | Jira Field | TraceRTM Field | Notes | |------------|----------------|-------| | Summary | title | Direct mapping | | Description | description | Converted from Atlassian format | | Issue Type | type | Maps to item types | | Status | status | Maps to item statuses | | Priority | metadata.priority | Stored in metadata | | Labels | metadata.labels | Array in met
headings: Export as CSV from Excel/Google Sheets

documents.description: Complete deployment and operations guide for TraceRTM
documents.content: # Deployment Guide Complete deployment and operations guide for TraceRTM in various environments. ## Quick Start ### Prerequisites - **Docker & Docker Compose** - Container runtime - **Python 3.12+** - Backend runtime - **Node.js 20+** - Frontend build - **PostgreSQL 15+** - Database - **Redis 7+** - Caching layer ### Local Development ### Docker Deployment ## Services ### PostgreSQL - **Port**: 5432 - **Database**: tracertm - **User**: tracertm - **Password**: Set via env var ### Redis - **Port**: 6379 - **Purpose**: Caching, sessions, rate limiting ### NATS - **Port**: 4222 (client), 8222 (monitoring) - **Purpose**: Event streaming, pub/sub ### API - **Port**: 8000 - **Endpoint**: http://localhost:8000 - **Docs**: http://localhost:8000/docs ## Configuration ### Environment Variables Create a file: ### Database Migrations Using Alembic for schema management: ## Production Deployment ### Using Docker Compose 1. **Update environment variables**: 2. **Build and start**: 3. **Run migratio

documents.title: MCP Tools Guide
documents.description: Using Model Context Protocol with TraceRTM
documents.content: # TraceRTM MCP/CLI/API Capability Matrix (Offline + Online) ## Scope This doc maps **MCP**, **CLI**, and **API** capabilities in both **offline** and **online** modes, and clarifies that each client is optional. ## Key Principles - **Offline-first:** Local DB is primary for local operations. - **Online optional:** Sync/API only required for remote collaboration. - **Client optionality:** MCP and CLI can be used independently (or together). - **Auth separation:** MCP auth does **not** depend on CLI state. ## Clients at a Glance ### MCP (FastMCP server) - **Entry:** or - **Auth:** WorkOS AuthKit JWT (OAuth), optional static dev API keys - **Offline:** Full CRUD + analysis via local DB - **Online:** Sync via API if configured ### CLI (Typer + Rich) - **Entry:** - **Auth:** WorkOS AuthKit device flow - **Offline:** Full CRUD + analysis via local DB - **Online:** Sync/pull/push via API ### API - **Local API client:** (DB-backed) - **Remote sync client:** (HTTP) - **Auth:** Bearer token (Aut

Create Feature Branch ### 2. Make Changes Follow TDD (Test-Driven Development): ### 3. Code Quality Run checks before committing: ### 4. Commit Changes Use conventional commits: ### 5. documents.title: TraceRTM Documentation
documents.description: Welcome to TraceRTM - Requirements Traceability & Management Platform
documents.content: # Welcome to TraceRTM TraceRTM is a comprehensive requirements traceability and management platform designed for modern software development teams. ## Quick Links - Getting Started - Set up TraceRTM and create your first project - Architecture - Understand the system design and components - Guides - Step-by-step guides for common tasks - API Reference - Complete API documentation and examples ## Features - **Multi-View System**: Manage requirements, code, tests, and APIs in unified views - **Cross-View Traceability**: Link items across different views for complete traceability - **Real-time Collaboration**: Work together with real-time updates via WebSocket - **GraphQL & REST APIs**: Flexible API access for integrations - **CLI & MCP Tools**: Command-line and Model Context Protocol tools for automation - **Enterprise-Ready**: Built with FastAPI, PostgreSQL, and React for scale ## Getting Started Start with our Quick Start Guide to get up and running in minutes. ## Architecture TraceRTM

documents.title: Backend Architecture
documents.description: FastAPI backend architecture and design patterns
documents.content: # Backend Architecture The TraceRTM backend is built with FastAPI following clean architecture principles with clear separation of concerns. ## Tech Stack ### Core Framework - **FastAPI** - Modern async web framework - **Python 3.12** - Latest Python with type hints - **Pydantic** - Data validation and serialization - **SQLAlchemy** - ORM for database operations - **Alembic** - Database migrations ### Infrastructure - **PostgreSQL** - Primary database - **Redis** - Caching layer - **NATS** - Event streaming - **uv** - Fast package manager ## Architecture Layers ### Layer 1: API Routes **Location**: FastAPI routers handling HTTP requests: ### Layer 2: Services **Location**: Business logic layer: ### Layer 3: Repositories **Location**: Data access layer: ### Layer 4: Models & Schemas **Models** () - SQLAlchemy ORM models: **Schemas** () - Pydantic validation models: ## Dependency Injection FastAPI's dependency injection system: ## Database Management ### Connection Pool ### Migrations Us

documents.title: Architecture
documents.description: System architecture and design decisions
documents.content: # TraceRTM Architecture TraceRTM is built with a modern, scalable architecture designed for enterprise requirements management. ## System Overview TraceRTM consists of several key components: - **Backend API**: FastAPI-based REST and GraphQL APIs - **Frontend Web**: React application with TanStack Router and Query - **Database**: PostgreSQL with SQLAlchemy ORM - **Real-time**: NATS and WebSocket for live updates - **CLI**: Command-line interface with Typer - **MCP**: Model Context Protocol for AI integration ## Architecture Principles ### 1. Clean Architecture We follow clean architecture principles with clear separation of concerns: - **Domain Layer**: Core business logic and entities - **Application Layer**: Use cases and services - **Infrastructure Layer**: Database, external services, APIs - **Presentation Layer**: Web UI, CLI, MCP tools ### 2. API-First Design All functionality is exposed through well-defined APIs: - **REST API**: For standard CRUD operations - **GraphQL API**: Fo

documents.title: Frontend Architecture
documents.description: React application architecture and design patterns
documents.content: # Frontend Architecture The TraceRTM frontend is a modern React application built with TypeScript, TanStack Router, and TanStack Query. ## Tech Stack ### Core Framework - **React 18** - UI framework with concurrent features - **TypeScript** - Type-safe development - **Vite** - Fast build tooling - **Tailwind CSS** - Utility-first styling - **shadcn/ui** - Accessible component library ### Routing & Data - **TanStack Router** - Type-safe file-based routing - **TanStack Query** - Server state management - **Zod** - Runtime validation ### Graph Visualization - **React Flow** - Interactive graph visualization - **D3.js** - Advanced visualizations ## Project Structure ## Architecture Patterns ### Component Organization Components follow a clear hierarchy: 1. **Pages** () - Route-level components 2. **Views** () - Feature-level components 3. **Components** () - Reusable UI components 4. **Primitives** () - Base shadcn/ui components ### State Management **Server State** (TanStack Query): - API

documents.title: CLI Architecture
documents.description: Command-line interface architecture and design patterns
documents.content: # TraceRTM CLI Architecture The TraceRTM CLI is built on **Typer** (FastAPI's CLI framework) with **Rich** for beautiful terminal output. The architecture follows a modular command group pattern, making it easy to extend and maintain. ## Architecture Layers ### Layer 1: Entry Point **File**: The main entry point that: - Initializes the Typer app - Registers all command groups - Handles global options (--version, --debug) - Sets up Rich console ### Layer 2: Command Groups **Location**: Each command group is a separate Typer app: - - Configuration management - - Project management - - Item CRUD operations - - Link management - - View switching - - Database operations - - Backup/restore - - Project state dashboard - - Cross-view search - - Drill-down navigation - - Stateless ingestion (MD/MDX/YAML) - - Data export - - Item history - - Performance benchmarking ### Layer 3: Services **Location**: Business logic is separated into services: - - Item operations - - Link operations - - View man

documents.title: Authentication
documents.description: Device-based OAuth 2.0 authentication flow
documents.content: # Authentication TraceRTM uses device-based OAuth 2.0 authentication, designed for CLI tools and desktop applications. ## Authentication Flow ## Endpoints ### Start Device Authorization Initiates the device authorization flow. **Request Body:** **Response:** **Code Samples:** ### Poll for Token Poll this endpoint to check if the user has completed authorization. **Request Body:** **Responses:** **Success (200):** **Pending (400):** **Expired (400):** ### Get Current User Returns information about the currently authenticated user. **Headers:** **Response:** **Code Sample:** ## Error Handling All authentication endpoints return standard error responses: Common error codes: - - Invalid client_id - - Missing or invalid parameters - - User hasn't authorized yet - - Too many polling requests - - Device code expired - - User denied authorization

documents.title: API Reference
documents.description: Complete TraceRTM REST API documentation with interactive examples
documents.content: # API Reference Welcome to the TraceRTM API reference documentation. This section provides comprehensive documentation for all available API endpoints, auto-generated from the backend OpenAPI specification. ## Overview The TraceRTM API is a RESTful API built with FastAPI that provides programmatic access to all traceability management features. All endpoints return JSON responses and follow standard HTTP status code conventions. ### Base URL ### Authentication Most endpoints require authentication. TraceRTM uses device-based OAuth 2.0 flow: 1. Request a device code from 2. User navigates to verification URL and enters the code 3. Poll to receive access token 4. Include token in header: See Authentication for detailed endpoint documentation. ## API Categories The API is organized into the following categories: ### Authentication & Authorization - Device authorization flow - Token management - User context ### Projects & Items - Project management - Requirements tracking - Test case mana
